---
title: "Labs"
toc: false
---

Step-by-step walkthroughs you can run on your own machine. Labs are
designed to be **self-serve**: read top to bottom, follow along, and
verify each step on your own machine.

Scheduled Mon/Wed class times are reserved for required checkpoint
conferences, optional demonstrations, troubleshooting, and open
studio work. You can work through these labs on your own, or bring
your in-progress work to one of the scheduled meetings in the MAC for
help.

## Available

- [Lab 1 — Install the stack (VS Code, R, Quarto, TinyTeX)](lab01-install-stack.qmd)
- [Lab 2 — First LaTeX math](lab02-first-latex-math.qmd) — write,
  render, and visually verify a short mathematical document in
  Quarto.
- [Lab 3 — Writing a Claim, Example, and Justification in Quarto](lab03-claim-example-justification.qmd) —
  structure a short mathematical writeup with a setup, claim, example,
  and justification, then inspect for logic, not just formatting.

## Planned

Labs are added as the course is built. The rough plan, aligned with
the weekly module spine:

- Lab 4 — Figures, tables, and a bibliography that compiles *(Week 4)*
- Lab 5 — A tour of a tidy dataset in R *(Week 7)*
- Lab 6 — A `ggplot2` walkthrough *(Week 8)*
- Lab 7 — A small reproducible simulation in R *(Week 9)*
- Lab 8 — Drafting and verifying technical prose with an AI assistant *(Weeks 11–12)*
- Lab 9 — A first portfolio folder, optional Git workflow *(Week 13)*

Lab numbering is rough — not every week has a paired lab.
